children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: In a case in which any Federal lands included within the park are legally occupied or utilized on , for grazing purposes, pursuant to a lease, permit, or license for a fixed term of years issued or authorized by any department, establishment, or agency of the United States, the Secretary of the Interior shall permit the persons holding such grazing privileges or their heirs to continue in the exercise thereof during the term of the lease, permit, or license, and one period of renewal thereafter.November 12, 1971

children.heading: Continuation of grazing leases, permits, and licenses
children.label: 1 Continuation of grazing leases, permits, and licenses
children.text: In the case of any grazing lease, permit, or license with respect to land in the Lost Spring Canyon Addition that was issued before , the Secretary shall, subject to periodic renewal, continue the grazing lease, permit, or license for a period equal to the lifetime of the holder of the grazing lease, permit, or license as of , plus the lifetime of any direct descendants of the holder born before .October 30, 1998October 30, 1998October 30, 1998

children.heading: Retirement
children.label: 2 Retirement
children.text: A grazing lease, permit, or license described in paragraph (1) shall be permanently retired at the end of the period described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Periodic renewal
children.label: 3 Periodic renewal
children.text: Until the expiration of the period described in paragraph (1), the holder (or descendant of the holder) of a grazing lease, permit, or license shall be entitled to renew the lease, permit, or license periodically, subject to such limitations, conditions, or regulations as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Sale
children.label: 4 Sale
children.text: A grazing lease, permit, or license described in paragraph (1) may be sold during the period described in paragraph (1) only on the condition that the purchaser shall, immediately upon acquisition, permanently retire the lease, permit, or license.

children.heading: Taylor Grazing Act
children.label: 5 Taylor Grazing Act
children.text: Nothing in this subsection affects other provisions concerning leases, permits, or licenses under the Act of (commonly known as the “Taylor Grazing Act”) (, chapter 865; et seq.).June 28, 193448 Stat. 126943 U.S.C. 315

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 6 Administration
children.text: Any portion of a grazing lease, permit, or license with respect to land in the Lost Spring Canyon Addition shall be administered by the National Park Service.
